title: Atelier subprocess egress audit record
description: One record per attempted network egress from a subprocess-launching tool call (today, the built-in `shell` tool). Spec §11 acceptance gate: `curl evil.example` blocked + logged; spec §12 makes the audit log exportable for privacy review. Lives next to the model-call audit record (`schemas/audit/egress.v1.json`) and shares the same on-disk `audit.log` file, discriminated by `kind`.

kind.description: Discriminator shared with `schemas/audit/egress.v1.json` (which carries `kind: "model-call"` once the model-call audit producer ships).

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 timestamp at the moment the egress attempt was detected by the harness.

tool_call_id.description: ID of the originating ToolCallRequest. Matches the id persisted in `session.json` so an audit row can be traced back to the originating model turn.

tool_name.description: Name of the originating built-in or MCP-routed tool (e.g., `shell`).

destination.description: Host (optionally with port) the subprocess attempted to reach. Parsed out of the command string. The full command is deliberately NOT recorded — see spec §12 privacy guarantees and the redaction policy.

outcome.description: Today always `blocked` — spec §11 default policy is deny-all egress. Reserved for a future `allowed` once the policy gains an allow-list mode.

reason.description: Why the harness enforced the block. Today always `sandbox-deny-net` (the §11 policy `allow_net: false` default).
